STUDIO X

Ideas turned into reality.

Search-to-enquiry drop-off reduced

Location and lifestyle imagery01 - 04

The Brief

A real estate experience that sells the feeling, not just the floorplan.

Company Two came to us with a familiar platform problem: users wanted to browse aspirational homes, but the product experience made that feel transactional, fragmented, and strangely exhausting.

We restructured the journey around emotional momentum. Search became simpler, filters became more human, and listings finally had space to create desire before demanding action. Maps, saved homes, and enquiry states all worked together instead of competing.

The result made buyers stay longer and act sooner. The platform felt premium without becoming precious, and practical without ever feeling cold.

What We Built

01R

Buyer Research

Persona and journey work focused on where confidence disappeared in the property search flow.

02F

Search & Filters

Criteria and sorting rebuilt around real decision habits, not legacy CMS constraints.

03M

Map Experience

A more usable geographic layer that keeps search context visible while exploring listings.

04L

Listing Design

Detail pages built to balance emotional imagery with practical next-step information.

05S

Saved Homes

Comparison and revisit patterns designed for buyers making careful long-cycle decisions.

06C

Agent Contact Flow

Enquiry states and forms streamlined to reduce hesitation at the moment of intent.

Premium discovery

"Good real-estate UX makes the decision feel calmer before it makes it faster."

How we rebuilt the search experience.

01
Discovery
We followed buyers through fragmented search journeys and identified exactly where outdated real-estate patterns were costing attention.
02
Strategy
The new direction focused on calm decision-making: richer listing previews, better filters, and tighter map interactions.
03
Design
Layouts, cards, and map components were tuned to feel aspirational without losing clarity or speed on mobile.
04
Delivery
We delivered a modular search system, responsive listing flows, and a scalable visual language for future growth.

Our users were dropping off at the search stage. We thought it was a product problem. It was a design problem. Fixed in one sprint.

D

Daniel W.
